name: 1p36_microduplication_recurrent_CNV
description: 1p36 subtelomeric microduplication syndrome — reciprocal CNV to the well-known 1p36 deletion. Variable-sized duplications (1.5-10 Mb) of the distal short arm, frequently mediated by subtelomeric SD blocks and non-recurrent NHEJ/MMBIR breakpoints (only a minority are pure NAHR-driven). Distinct from the more penetrant deletion in phenotype.

architecture.internal_structure: Subtelomeric SD cluster shared with other acrocentric/subtelomeric regions; distal core contains MMP23B, GABRD, SKI, PRDM16, RERE, MTHFR, GNB1. Most reported duplications are non-recurrent in size, suggesting MMBIR/FoSTeS rather than canonical NAHR.
